results.title: Music of the spheres
results.creator_credit: Adrian Malone, Dick Gilling, BBC-TV and Time-Life Films
results.brief_description: Jacob Bronowski discusses the influence of mathematics and numbers on culture throughout the ages. Going back to the time of Pythagorus, he demonstrates how his mathematical formula influenced Greek music and thought; how it was adapted in Alexandria by people such as Ptolemy; the development of symmetry art and culture; to the translation of numerical thought into Latin and its effect on perspective, depth and dimension in art. Throughout, it is stressed by Bronowski how the inner eye of the painter and the logic of the mathematician have combined in the development of art and culture. Written and narrated by Jacob Bronowski. Suitable for middle and upper secondary levels.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Adrian Malone, Dick Gilling, BBC-TV and Time-Life Films

results.title: Music of India: instrumental
results.creator_credit: A. Bhaskar Rao, M. Bhavnani and Films Division, Government of India
results.brief_description: A number of musical instruments are shown. Saraswati veena is a favourite stringed instrument of Southern India. Denai is a reed instrument, usually played in company with several other instruments. Sursazar is a complicated string instrument; the strings are plucked with the left hand and bowed with the right, at the same time. Sitar is a plucked string instrument, and one of the best known of Indian instruments. Tabla and baya are drums, played together, one with each hand; they are mostly used for accompaniment, but sometimes are played alone.

results.title: Music lessons (TEFC)
results.brief_description: Describes how the Kodaly method of music instruction is being applied in American schools. The progression of learning; the training required to teach the method; the preparation for each lesson, and the commitment schools must make if they choose to adopt this method.

results.title: Music in the school
results.creator_credit: Margaretha Rosencrantz, Royal Swedish Board of Education and Svensk Filmindustri
results.brief_description: The teaching of music in Swedish schools. The place of music in the curriculum and as one of the outside interests provided for by the schools. The methods used in teaching singing, instrumental work, music appreciation, theory and history at all levels are shown in some detail. The soundtrack is in English except for children singing.

results.title: Music from Montreal
results.creator_credit: David Bairstow, Peter Jones and ONF | NFB
results.brief_description: A film about the Montreal Bach Choir and its dynamic director, George Little. The choir is heard at rehearsal and in excerpts from a performance of Bach’s Christmas Oratorio.

results.title: Mushrooms = Champignons
results.creator_credit: Pierre Veilleux, Robert Forget and ONF | NFB
results.brief_description: Mushrooms are deceiving. They can play tricks with your head. In this animation film, mushrooms are a powerful metaphor. Their metamorphosis suggests that life is a vast “food chain”. If this is indeed our reality, can we, the consumers, escape being consumed? A film that serves up a riveting “plat du jour”. Awards: Zagreb; Chicago

results.title: Mushroom 25 live: Telstra concert of the century
results.brief_description: On Saturday the 14th of November 1998, some of Australia’s most popular performers gathered to celebrate 25 years of the Mushroom recording label- thousands of fans representing a multitude of generations packed into the Melbourne Cricket Ground for the musical spectacular. This three video set features almost six hours of live performances by the greatest names in Australian music including: Moler, Machinations, Scott Carne, Painters & Dockers, Nick Barker, Paul Norton, Wendy Stapleton, Stars, Dynamic Hepnotics, The Badloves, Chain, Juno Roxus, Dave Warner, The Ferrets, Weddings Parties Anything, Chris Wilson, Wilson Diesel, Vika and Linda, Madder Lake, Chantoozies, James Freud, Swoop, Christie Allen, Uncanny X-Men, Dannii and Kylie Minogue, The Mavis’s, Deborah Conway, Archie Roach, Ruby Hunter, Mark Seymour, Chris Bailey, Paul Kelly, Renee Geyer, The Sports, Mick Molloy, Sunnyboys, Ol‘55, Billy Thorpe, Choirboys, Ian Moss, Kate Ceberano, Peter Andre, Deni Hines, Leonardo’s Bride, The Whitlams, The Angels, Jimmy Barnes, and INXS.

results.title: Museum Visitor
results.creator_credit: Konstantin Lopushansky, Channel 2 (ZDF) West Germany, Gosko and ZSM Film Produktion
results.brief_description: A remarkable fable warning of ecological catastrophe. Max decides to visit a museum on the bottom of the seabed, one which can only be reached during the seven-day low tide. What he finds while he waits, however, is a place reserved for mutants, victims of an ecological catastrophe.
